name: Stream Chat API
what_it_does: Provides managed in-app chat infrastructure with channels, threads, reactions, and real-time delivery via REST API and WebSocket, using API key + secret HMAC authentication and JWT user tokens for client sessions.
use_cases: Send automated messages into Stream channels from agent workflows to notify users of task completions or alerts
Query channel message history to feed conversation context into LLM summarization or analysis pipelines
Manage channel membership programmatically to add or remove users based on business logic events
Consume Stream webhooks to trigger agent actions when users send messages or react to content
Create and configure channel types with custom permissions for agent-managed chat rooms in product applications
not_for: General-purpose event streaming or IoT telemetry — Stream Chat is designed for human conversation patterns, not high-frequency sensor data
Email or SMS notification delivery — Stream operates within its own in-app channel construct only
Deployments requiring fully self-hosted infrastructure with no third-party data processing
best_when: Best when building or extending a product that already uses Stream Chat and an agent needs to read, write, or react to channel activity via the server-side API.
avoid_when: Avoid when the use case is purely pub/sub data routing without a chat UX layer, as Stream's pricing and data model are optimized for conversation-centric applications.

auth.notes: Server-side calls use API key + API secret for HMAC request signing. Client-side SDKs use short-lived JWT user tokens generated server-side from the API secret. No OAuth flow. JWTs must be refreshed before expiry.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier includes up to 5 million messages/month and 100 MAU; suitable for development and small apps
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter ~$299/month; Pro ~$599/month; Scale and Enterprise tiers with custom pricing for high MAU
pricing.notes: Pricing is message-volume and MAU based. The free tier is among the most generous in the category. Advanced features like push notifications and moderation require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits are 3,000 requests/minute on standard plans; burst handling is allowed with documented backoff behavior

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Stream returns well-structured JSON error objects with numeric codes, human-readable messages, and documentation links in some error types. SDK error wrapping is consistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message sends can include a custom id field to prevent duplicates — channel-level deduplication occurs if the same message ID is sent twice within a window.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT user tokens have a short default expiry; agents operating long-lived connections must implement proactive token refresh or face mid-session authentication failures
HMAC request signing must be computed correctly — small errors in header format produce opaque 401 errors without hints about what is wrong
Webhook delivery is at-least-once; agent webhook handlers must be idempotent to avoid duplicate processing of message events
Channel query filters use a non-standard JSON syntax that differs from SQL and MongoDB conventions, causing subtle query logic bugs
Rate limit responses return 429 with a Retry-After header but SDK retry logic is not always enabled by default in all language clients

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. HIPAA BAA available. API secret must be protected server-side — leaking it allows impersonation of any user. JWT tokens are short-lived which limits blast radius of token leaks.
